reasoning: The unsafe findings in qwen's report (install-rpm.sh, install.sh, extract-cowork-svc.sh) are packaging scripts for other distributions or development tools not executed during AUR build/install. PKGBUILD and .install scripts are safe.
disagreement_analysis: qwen misinterpreted distribution-specific packaging scripts as AUR threats. deepseek correctly focused on AUR-relevant execution context.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped claude-cowork-service-1.0.62.tar.gz source archive. While checksum-verified, deeper binary inspection might be warranted.
qwen missed src/claude-cowork-service-1.0.62/vm/qemu.go (VM management) and preflight.go reviewed by deepseek
false_positives: qwen: install-rpm.sh (RPM-specific script not used in AUR build)
qwen: install.sh (APT helper irrelevant to Arch)
qwen: extract-cowork-svc.sh (development tool not invoked by PKGBUILD)
false_negatives: deepseek noted command-injection risk in build-pkg.sh (though not malicious) - qwen missed this
learnings: File selection: Prioritize files referenced in PKGBUILD (prepare/build/package) and .install scripts. Deprioritize distro-specific packaging/CI scripts.
Prompt improvement: Clarify that non-AUR packaging scripts are out-of-scope unless executed during makepkg.
Common failure: Models confuse development/reverse-engineering tools with malicious behavior. Add heuristics to flag 'curl|bash' only if used in PKGBUILD/.install.
